content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


May 10, 1989


Honorable Maxine Waters
California State Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-261

Dear Assemblywoman Waters:

You have requested advice concerning your responsibilities
under the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

This letter confirms that your letter dated April 20, 1989,
accurately summarizes the verbal advice I provided you on
April 19th regarding mailing of announcements to your constituents
of the Cinco de Mayo event cosponsored by you and the Employment
Development Department. A copy of your incoming letter is
enclosed for your convenience.

Please contact me at 322-5901 if you have any further
questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Lilly Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:LS:ld

REPLY TO

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO, CA 94249-0001
(916) 445-2363

LOS ANGELES ADDRESS
7900 SOUTH CENTRAL AVE
LOS ANGELES, CA 90001
(213) 582-7371

Assembly
California Legislature

COMMITTEES:
GOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATION
EDUCATION
NATURAL RESOURCES
WAYS AND MEANS
JOINT LEGISLATIVE BUDGET COMMITTEE

SUBCOMMITTEE:
CHAIRWOMAN, SUBCOMMITTEE #4
ON STATE ADMINISTRATION

MAXINE WATERS
ASSEMBLYWOMAN, FORTY-EIGHTH DISTRICT

DEMOCRATIC CAUCUS CHAIRWOMAN

April 20, 1989

Ms. Lilly Spitz
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Lilly:

This letter is to confirm our conversation of yesterday
regarding the application of the mass mailing prohibitions of the
political reform act to an official agency event being sponsored
by the Employment Development Department and myself.

We are planning a Cinco de Mayo official agency event for the
purpose of informing my constituents of the government services
available to them on the state and local level. I understand that
regulation 18901 (f) (10) allows us to mail announcements of the
Cinco de Mayo official agency event to my constituents without
limitation, so long as the notice specifies only the time, date,
place, and description of the subject matter of the meeting. My
name may appear once on this notice.

If this is in accord with your memory of our conversation, I
would appreciate confirmation of this letter, in writing as soon
as possible.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

MAXINE WATERS
ASSEMBLYWOMAN, 48TH DISTRICT

MW/RSC/rj

Assembly

California Legislature

MAXINE WATERS
ASSEMBLYWOMAN, FORTY-EIGHTH DISTRICT

DEMOCRATIC CAUCUS CHAIRWOMAN


April 20, 1989


Ms. Lilly Spitz
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Lilly:

     This letter is to confirm our conversation of yesterday
regarding the application of the mass mailing prohibitions of the
political reform act to an official agency event being sponsored
by the Employment Development Department and myself.

     We are planning a Cinco de Mayo official agency event for the
purpose of informing my constituents of the government services
available to them on the state and local level. I understand that
regulation 18901 (f) (10) allows us to mail announcements of the
Cinco de Mayo official agency event to my constituents without
limitation, so long as the notice specifies only the time, date,
place, and description of the subject matter of the meeting. My
name may appear once on this notice.

     If this is in accord with your memory of our conversation, I
would appreciate confirmation of this letter, in writing as soon
as possible.

     Thank you for your assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        MAXINE WATERS
MW/RSC/rj                              ASSEMBLYWOMAN, 48TH DISTRICT

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


May 10, 1989


Honorable Maxine Waters
California State Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-261

Dear Assemblywoman Waters:

You have requested advice concerning your responsibilities
under the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

This letter confirms that your letter dated April 20, 1989,
accurately summarizes the verbal advice I provided you on
April 19th regarding mailing of announcements to your constituents
of the Cinco de Mayo event cosponsored by you and the Employment
Development Department.  A copy of your incoming letter is
enclosed for your convenience.

Please contact me at 322-5901 if you have any further
questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Lild Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division
